description_long: This object consists of a cluster of four polygonal rocks with a stylized, faceted appearance and a warm, reddish-brown coloration. The surfaces are mostly flat with some angular edges and visible cracks, giving the rocks a weathered and natural look. The largest two rocks are positioned at the back, while two smaller rocks sit in the foreground, creating a sense of depth and grouping. Subtle highlights and shading suggest a matte, slightly reflective surface, while the cracks and chips add visual interest and realism. The rocks appear solid and heavy, with no visible openings or cavities. Their overall form and finish suggest they could serve as decorative or functional barriers in an urban park or landscaped area.
description: A group of four reddish-brown, faceted rocks with visible cracks and a matte, slightly reflective surface, arranged in a natural cluster.
description_view_0: The front view shows all four rocks, with the two largest at the back and two smaller ones in front, highlighting the cracks and angular surfaces.
description_view_1: This side view emphasizes the height of the largest rock and reveals more of the cracks and facets on its surface.
description_view_2: From this angle, only the two largest rocks are visible, showing their broad, flat faces and subtle surface details.
description_view_3: This view displays the tops of the rocks, with the largest rock in the foreground and the smaller rocks partially obscured behind.
attribute_location: urban park or landscaped public space
attribute_integration_with_urban_infrast: There is no visible evidence of intentional integration with urban infrastructure.
